message:
fix(rate-limit): add atomic tryConsume() to prevent race condition bypass

- Add tryConsume() method to InMemoryRateLimiter that atomically checks
  and increments in a single operation
- Update rateLimitMiddleware to use tryConsume() instead of the vulnerable
  isLimited() + check() pattern that allowed concurrent requests to bypass
  the rate limit
- Extract enforceMaxItems() helper for code reuse
- Add comprehensive tests for concurrent access scenarios:
  - tryConsume atomicity tests
  - Concurrent request limiting via Promise.all
  - High burst scenario with 100 concurrent requests

Fixes bd-11wh: The old pattern checked isLimited() then processed the
request, then incremented. Multiple concurrent requests could all pass
the isLimited() check before any counter was incremented, effectively
bypassing the rate limit during burst traffic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
